rule_name: S3 Bucket Policy Changed to Public Access
description: Detects when an S3 bucket policy is modified to allow public access (Principal: *). Public S3 buckets are one of the most common causes of cloud data breaches. An attacker may make a bucket public to exfiltrate data or an insider may accidentally expose sensitive information.
triage_steps: Determine what data is in the bucket — is it sensitive?
Check if the bucket is tagged as intentionally public (e.g., static website hosting)
Review the source IP and user identity for signs of compromise
false_positive_notes: Legitimate public buckets include: static website hosting, public dataset distribution, CDN origin buckets. Maintain a whitelist of approved public buckets and exclude them from alerting.
tuning_guidance: Add exclusions for known public-by-design buckets. Consider a two-stage alert: first detect the policy change, then check if the policy actually contains public access (requires parsing requestParameters).
response_actions: IMMEDIATELY remove the public policy or ACL
Enable S3 Block Public Access at the account level to prevent future occurrences
Audit the bucket's access logs to determine if data was accessed while public
Investigate the identity that made the change
Report the exposure window to your security team and compliance
